chunk_summary: Guidelines for standardized admission orders and monitoring charts in the first 24–72 hours of inpatient SAM care to prevent omissions and iatrogenic harm.
learning_objectives: Understand the core elements of standardized admission orders for SAM management.
Learn the key variables to monitor for early detection of deterioration in SAM patients.
Recognize escalation rules and triggers for senior review in SAM care.
Familiarize with the use of F-75 feeding and empiric antibiotics in initial stabilization.
key_facts: Admission orders should include glucose checks and treatment if <54 mg/dL or symptomatic.
F-75 feeding should start immediately, based on weight, every 2 hours day and night.
Empiric antibiotics are recommended for complicated SAM cases.
Monitoring charts must track temperature, pulse, respiratory rate, mental status, and feed intake.
Escalation triggers include intake <80% of prescribed volume, recurrent hypoglycemia, and failure to regain appetite by day 4.
Routine potassium and magnesium supplementation with sodium restriction is advised.
reference_quote: Use a pre-printed or electronic admission order set and a minimum monitoring chart so that feeding, warming, and antibiotic steps occur without delay. (Source: WHO, Pocket book of hospital care for children)
document_key_topics: Monitoring chart variables and escalation rules
